title: Ethereal Reverie II
location: Juruo Golden Beach Gallery · Quanzhou · China
theme:
Ethereal Reverie II marks my second solo exhibition, set along the breathtaking Golden Coast of Quanzhou. The venue itself feels almost like an extension of the exhibition—a pink, castle-like structure perched by the sea. Through its expansive floor-to-ceiling windows, waves can be seen crashing gently against the building, creating a setting that is both intimate and romantic.

The building’s soft palette and cinematic atmosphere inevitably call to mind the visual language of Wes Anderson. I was instantly drawn to it at first sight; its dreamlike quality resonates deeply with the spirit of Ethereal Reverie.

This exhibition presents a total of sixteen works, including Mother of Universe, alongside several additional realist oil paintings that were not part of the original Ethereal Reverie series. Together, they form a dialogue between the imagined and the observed, expanding the narrative of the series into a broader emotional and visual landscape.
